what_it_does: This noticeboard item documents a design for a two-way reply path that lets Claude send replies back to an external app by registering an MCP reply tool, injecting instructions into the system prompt, and sending outbound replies via POST or SSE, with a mention of remote permission relay for tool-use approval.
observations: Proposes adding a tools:{} capability and registering a standard MCP tool such as reply(chat_id, text).
Recommends putting an instructions string in the server constructor to tell Claude when to reply and which meta attribute to route back on.
Describes Claude calling the reply tool and a handler sending the reply OUT via POST to the platform or an SSE broadcast.
Notes the docs' local example uses SSE on GET /events so curl -N can watch replies live, and this is the model for pushing replies to the project's browser app.
Mentions a permission relay 'claude/channel/permission' available in v2.1.81+ for remote tool-use approval, gated by sender.
It is a block-type noticeboard item in the dragnet-development channel, linked as part_of board://item-8798b611, filed by ch-3mpkjg3r on 2026-06-24.
standouts: Two-way reply path design: external app ← Claude.
MCP tool reply(chat_id, text) plus instructions injected into the system prompt.
Outbound reply delivery modeled as POST or SSE, with SSE used as the browser-app push model.
Remote permission relay for approving tool use remotely.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter convention with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a Markdown heading and bullet list for free-form design notes.
References a versioned feature (v2.1.81+) and a specific internal channel/topic name.
concerns: The 'standard MCP tool' is asserted without showing a schema, capability shape, or error handling.
The 'meta attr to route back on' is not named, so the routing key remains unspecified.
SSE on GET /events is presented as a docs example without security, scaling, or subscription details.
The permission relay mention is brief and does not explain sender gating mechanics.
notes: Design note for adding a two-way reply path from Claude back to an external app using an MCP reply tool, injected instructions, and POST/SSE outbound delivery, with a mention of remote permission relay.
questions: Which specific meta attribute should be used to route replies back to the correct chat?
What is the exact MCP capability declaration and JSON schema for reply(chat_id, text)?
How is the instructions string formatted and injected into the system prompt?
What is the actual platform endpoint or SSE channel the browser app consumes?
What does 'sender gating' mean for claude/channel/permission approval?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to describe the reply path (two-way), and the content matches by outlining tool registration, instructions injection, outbound delivery, and permission relay.
apparent_themes: Bidirectional integration between Claude and external applications
Tool-use and MCP as extension mechanisms
intention_signals: It is trying to specify how to let Claude initiate replies back to an external app or browser, using MCP tools and server-injected instructions, while keeping remote tool-use approval in mind.
novelty: It frames the reply path as a standard MCP tool with injected reply instructions and uses SSE as the live push model for a browser app, rather than a simple request-response API.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete two-way messaging design pattern: an MCP reply tool, system-prompt instructions for routing, and SSE/POST outbound delivery, plus a pointer to remote permission relay for tool-use approval.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note specifying a two-way reply path from an external app to Claude using an MCP reply tool, injected system-prompt instructions, and POST or SSE outbound delivery, with a note on remote permission relay.
intention_for_embedding: To document how to enable Claude to send replies back to an external app or browser via MCP tools and event streaming.
